fields.title: Redesign personal website
fields.goal: Create a clean, modern portfolio site to showcase recent work and attract freelance clients

fields.title: Plan summer trip to Japan
fields.goal: Organize a 2-week trip through Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka for August

fields.title: Book flights and accommodation
Plan day-by-day itinerary
Home office ergonomic overhaul
fields.goal: Set up a comfortable, healthy workspace to reduce back pain during long work sessions

fields.title: Learn Mediterranean cooking
fields.goal: Build a repertoire of 10 healthy Mediterranean dishes I can cook confidently

fields.title: Build emergency fund
fields.goal: Save 6 months of living expenses in a high-yield savings account

fields.title: Organize digital photo archive
fields.goal: Sort 10 years of photos into folders by year/event and back up to external drive

fields.title: Read 'Thinking, Fast and Slow'
Prepare workshop on personal productivity
fields.goal: Design a 90-minute interactive workshop on GTD basics for the team at work

fields.description: Buy domain name on Namecheap
Research static site generators (Hugo, Astro, 11ty)
Wireframe homepage and project gallery layout
Write copy for the About page
Set up Plausible analytics
Ask two friends to review the draft before launch
Renew passport at the post office
Apply for travel insurance
Exchange currency at the bank
Compare round-trip flight prices on Google Flights
Book Airbnb in Shinjuku for the first week
Reserve a ryokan in Kyoto for three nights
Look into Osaka hostel options near Dotonbori
Research day trips from Tokyo (Kamakura, Nikko)
List must-visit temples in Kyoto
Find a sushi-making class in Osaka
Map out JR Pass coverage for inter-city travel
Buy a desk lamp with warm light
Order sit-stand desk from FlexiSpot
Pick a monitor arm that fits a 27" display
Install under-desk cable management tray
Try out a split keyboard (Kinesis or ZSA)
Stock up on olive oil, tahini, and spice basics
Try the shakshuka recipe from Ottolenghi
Practice making fresh pasta from scratch
Find a good farmers market for weekend shopping
Cook a full mezze spread for Saturday dinner
Review and cancel unused subscriptions
Open a high-yield savings account
Set up automatic monthly transfer of 500 EUR
Revisit budget spreadsheet at end of month
Download all photos from Google Photos
Sort into year/event folder structure
Delete duplicates with dupeGuru
Copy final archive to external SSD
Borrow from the library or buy Kindle version
Outline the three main sections of the talk
Create slide deck with key concepts and examples
Prepare a hands-on exercise for the capture step
Book the meeting room for March 15th
Send calendar invite to all participants
Call dentist to schedule annual checkup
Buy a birthday gift for Sarah's party on Saturday
Fix the leaking kitchen faucet
Drop off winter clothes at the donation bin
Reply to the email from the landlord about the lease renewal

fields.title: Start a small vegetable garden on the balcony
fields.notes: Tomatoes, herbs, maybe some chili peppers. Check if the balcony gets enough sunlight.

fields.title: Learn basic woodworking
fields.notes: There is a makerspace downtown that offers weekend intro courses. Could start with a simple shelf.

fields.title: Take an online photography course
fields.notes: Interested in street photography. Check courses on Domestika or Skillshare.

fields.title: Volunteer at the local animal shelter
fields.notes: They need help on Saturday mornings. Would be a nice way to spend time outdoors.

fields.title: Write a short story
fields.notes: Have had an idea about a time traveler stuck in a library. Just need to sit down and draft it.

fields.title: Build a home automation system with Raspberry Pi
fields.notes: Start with smart lighting and a temperature sensor. Home Assistant looks like the right platform.

fields.title: Learn to play the ukulele
fields.notes: Seems easier to pick up than guitar. There's a second-hand one for sale on the neighborhood board.

fields.title: Start a book club with friends
fields.notes: Monthly meetups, rotating who picks the book. Ask Marco and Elisa if they are interested.

fields.title: Train for a half marathon
fields.notes: Knee has been bothering me lately. Probably better to focus on swimming first.

fields.title: Try bouldering at the local climbing gym
fields.notes: Good full-body workout. The gym near the station has day passes for 12 EUR.

fields.title: Learn to bake sourdough bread
fields.notes: Need to get a starter going first. Ask Laura if she can share some of hers.

fields.title: Set up a personal wiki or digital garden
fields.notes: Obsidian, Notion, or maybe just a static site with wiki links. Would help organize notes.

fields.title: Take a weekend trip to the Dolomites
fields.notes: Autumn colors would be amazing. Check rifugio availability for a one-night hike.

fields.title: Learn to develop black-and-white film at home
fields.notes: Starter kit with chemicals and a developing tank costs around 50 EUR. Would be fun with the old Pentax.

fields.title: Read the complete works of Italo Calvino
fields.notes: Already read If on a winter's night a traveler. Start with Invisible Cities next.

fields.title: Build a custom mechanical keyboard
fields.notes: Cherry MX Brown switches, 65% layout. Check r/MechanicalKeyboards for build guides.

fields.title: Organize a neighborhood cleanup day
fields.notes: Could post on the building WhatsApp group. Spring would be the best time.

fields.title: Try pottery or ceramics classes
fields.notes: There is a studio on Via Roma that offers evening beginner courses.

fields.title: Learn basic car maintenance
fields.notes: At least how to change oil, replace wiper blades, and check tire pressure properly.

fields.title: Set up a proper backup system for all devices
fields.notes: NAS with RAID? Or just a cloud solution like Backblaze. Need to protect photos and documents.

fields.title: Watch all of Kubrick's films in chronological order
fields.notes: Start with Fear and Desire (1953). Could do one per week.

fields.title: Learn to swim butterfly stroke
fields.notes: Already comfortable with freestyle and backstroke. The pool offers adult technique sessions on Tuesdays.

fields.title: Build a small lending library box for the building entrance
fields.notes: Wooden box, weatherproof, mounted on the wall. Fill with books I've already read.

fields.title: Start a daily journaling habit
fields.notes: Morning pages or evening reflection. Just 10 minutes. Try it for 30 days first.

fields.title: Visit the thermal baths in Saturnia
fields.notes: Free natural hot springs in southern Tuscany. Best to go on a weekday to avoid crowds.

fields.title: Learn to use a sewing machine
fields.notes: Mom has an old Singer she never uses. Would be great for basic repairs and alterations.

fields.title: Try intermittent fasting for a month
fields.notes: 16:8 window. Skip breakfast, eat between noon and 8 PM. Read more about the science first.

fields.title: Contribute to an open source project
fields.notes: Look for good-first-issue tags on GitHub. Something in Python or JavaScript would be ideal.

fields.title: Declutter the apartment room by room
fields.notes: Follow the KonMari method. Start with clothes, then books, then miscellaneous.

fields.title: Take a first aid course
fields.notes: Red Cross offers weekend courses. Good skill to have, and the certificate lasts 3 years.

fields.title: Build a standing desk from IKEA parts
fields.notes: Saw a hack using IVAR shelving and a LINNMON top. Much cheaper than a motorized one.

fields.title: Learn to solve a Rubik's cube
fields.notes: Start with the beginner method, then move to CFOP. There are great YouTube tutorials.

fields.title: Explore the abandoned railway line trail on bike
fields.notes: The old Ferrovia della Val di Fiemme has been converted to a bike path. About 50 km round trip.

fields.title: Make homemade limoncello
fields.notes: Need organic lemons, grain alcohol, sugar, and patience. Makes a great gift.

fields.title: Learn chess openings properly
fields.notes: Know the basics but always get crushed in the middlegame. Try the Lichess studies.

fields.title: Go stargazing somewhere with no light pollution
fields.notes: Check the dark sky map. Maybe the hills south of town. Need to borrow binoculars at least.

fields.title: Set up a fish tank with live plants
fields.notes: Low-tech planted tank. Neon tetras, shrimp, and some java fern. 60-liter would be enough.

fields.title: Try a digital detox weekend
fields.notes: No phone, no laptop from Friday evening to Sunday morning. Just books and walks.

fields.title: Paint the bedroom walls a warmer color
fields.notes: Something like a warm gray or soft terracotta. Need to move furniture first.

fields.title: Try a coworking space for a week
fields.notes: Talent Garden has day passes. Might help with focus and meet interesting people.

fields.title: Learn to make cocktails at home
fields.notes: Start with the classics: Negroni, Old Fashioned, Spritz. Need a proper jigger and shaker.

fields.title: Take a calligraphy workshop
fields.notes: Italic hand or brush lettering. Could be nice for handwritten cards and notes.

fields.title: Visit the Uffizi Gallery in Florence
fields.notes: Haven't been since school. Book tickets online to skip the queue. Combine with a day trip.

fields.title: Try meal prepping on Sundays
fields.notes: Cook 3-4 dishes in bulk for the week. Would save time and money on weekday lunches.

fields.title: Learn to identify local birds by song
fields.notes: Download the Merlin Bird ID app. Start with common garden birds: robin, blackbird, tit.

fields.title: Build a small bookshelf from reclaimed wood
fields.notes: The pallet wood behind the supermarket would work. Just need sandpaper and varnish.

fields.title: Sign up for a wine tasting course
fields.notes: The enoteca on Corso Italia does monthly tastings. Start with local wines from Trentino.

fields.title: Digitize old family photo albums
fields.notes: Borrow a flatbed scanner or use the Google PhotoScan app. Start with grandma's albums.

fields.title: Try a meditation retreat
fields.notes: Even a one-day silent retreat could be interesting. Check Plum Village or local Zen centers.

fields.title: Learn to sharpen kitchen knives properly
fields.notes: Get a whetstone (1000/3000 grit combo). There are good technique videos on YouTube.

fields.title: Run a tabletop RPG campaign for friends
fields.notes: D&D 5e or something lighter like Blades in the Dark. Would need to prep a short adventure first.

fields.title: Adopt a cat from the shelter
fields.notes: Would need to cat-proof the balcony and get the landlord's written permission first.

fields.title: Learn basic electronics with Arduino
fields.notes: Starter kit with breadboard and LEDs. Could eventually build a weather station.

fields.title: Do a bike tour along the Adige river
fields.notes: The cycle path from Bolzano to Verona is about 150 km. Could do it in two days with an overnight stop.

fields.title: Learn to fold origami beyond paper cranes
fields.notes: Modular origami looks incredible. Start with a Sonobe cube.

fields.title: Subscribe to a CSA farm box
fields.notes: Community-supported agriculture. Weekly box of seasonal vegetables delivered to the door.

fields.title: Try ice skating at the winter rink
fields.notes: The outdoor rink in Piazza Fiera is open until March. Skate rental is only 5 EUR.

fields.title: Research getting solar panels for the apartment
fields.notes: Balcony solar panels are a thing now. 600W plug-in systems don't need permits in most cases.

fields.title: Learn to play a song on the piano
fields.notes: There's a digital piano at the community center. Even just one piece â€” Clair de Lune would be the dream.

fields.title: Build a spice rack for the kitchen
fields.notes: Wall-mounted, so it doesn't take counter space. Could use small IKEA shelves or build from scratch.

fields.title: Visit a planetarium show
fields.notes: The science museum has a new show about deep space. Friday evening sessions.

fields.title: Try stand-up paddleboarding on the lake
fields.notes: Rentals available at Lago di Caldonazzo in summer. Looks very peaceful.

fields.title: Make a timelapse video of the sunset from the balcony
fields.notes: Use the phone with a small tripod. One photo every 10 seconds for an hour should work.

fields.title: Learn to make fresh mozzarella
fields.notes: Just need milk, citric acid, and rennet. There are 30-minute recipes online.

fields.title: Explore the local flea market on the first Sunday
fields.notes: Piazza del Mercato, first Sunday of each month. Might find interesting vintage stuff.

fields.title: Create a photo wall in the hallway
fields.notes: Print 10-15 favorite photos in black and white. Use matching frames from IKEA.

fields.title: Try cross-country skiing this winter
fields.notes: Rentals and groomed trails at LavazĂ¨ pass. Much cheaper than downhill skiing.

fields.title: Read GĂ¶del, Escher, Bach cover to cover
fields.notes: Tried once and got stuck in chapter 4. Maybe with a reading group it would be more manageable.

fields.title: Ferment homemade kimchi
fields.notes: Napa cabbage, gochugaru, fish sauce. Needs a fermentation jar. Ready in about a week.

fields.title: Take the train to Vienna for a long weekend
fields.notes: Direct Eurocity from Bolzano, about 4.5 hours. Visit museums, coffee houses, and the Naschmarkt.

fields.title: Set up a proper password manager
fields.notes: Bitwarden is free and open source. Need to migrate from the browser's saved passwords.

fields.title: Learn to draw faces and portraits
fields.notes: Drawabox and Proko have free structured lessons. Just need a sketchbook and pencils.

fields.title: Try a silent reading party
fields.notes: People gather at a bar, read in silence for an hour, then chat. Saw one advertised at Bookique.

fields.title: Improve touch typing speed
fields.notes: Currently around 60 WPM. Keybr.com and monkeytype are good free tools. Aim for 80+.

fields.title: Cook a full Thanksgiving dinner for friends
fields.notes: Not traditional here, but could be fun. Turkey, stuffing, cranberry sauce, pumpkin pie.

fields.title: Research switching to a cooperative bank
fields.notes: Banca Etica or a local credit union. Check fees, services, and how easy the migration is.

fields.title: Try geocaching on a weekend walk
fields.notes: Download the app and see what's hidden nearby. Could make regular walks more interesting.

fields.title: Build a rain garden or small pond in the courtyard
fields.notes: Would need the condo association's approval. A half-barrel water feature might be easier.

fields.title: Learn to tie useful knots
fields.notes: Bowline, clove hitch, trucker's hitch, sheet bend. Useful for camping and everyday life.

fields.title: Watch all the films that won the Palme d'Or
fields.notes: Could do one per week. Start from recent winners and go back in time.

fields.title: Try cold water swimming in the lake
fields.notes: Supposed to be great for circulation and mood. Start in autumn when it's still not too cold.

fields.title: Make homemade pasta shapes beyond spaghetti
fields.notes: Orecchiette, cavatelli, trofie. Just flour and water, shaped by hand. Very meditative.

fields.title: Attend a local council meeting
fields.notes: They're open to the public. Good to know what's being decided about the neighborhood.

fields.title: Learn enough German to have basic conversations
fields.notes: Living near the border, it would be practical. Duolingo to start, then maybe a VHS course.

fields.title: Try foraging for wild herbs and mushrooms
fields.notes: Need a proper guidebook first. Never eat anything without 100% identification. Join a guided walk.

fields.title: Set up a compost bin on the balcony
fields.notes: Bokashi system might work for a small space. No smell, and produces fertilizer for the plants.

fields.title: Restore the old wooden chair from the attic
fields.notes: Sand it down, fix the wobbly leg, and apply a new finish. Could be a nice weekend project.

fields.title: Map all the public drinking fountains in town
fields.notes: Take photos and GPS coordinates, then add them to OpenStreetMap. Useful for runners too.

fields.title: Try speed cubing competitions
fields.notes: First learn to solve consistently under 2 minutes, then optimize. Local meetups exist.

fields.title: Plant a tree somewhere
fields.notes: Some organizations let you sponsor and plant one locally. Or ask the comune about public spaces.

fields.title: Learn to do a handstand
fields.notes: Start against the wall. There are 30-day progressions online. Good for core strength too.

fields.title: Visit every museum in town at least once
fields.notes: There are at least 8 I haven't been to. Many have free entry on the first Sunday of the month.

fields.title: Record a field recording of city sounds
fields.notes: Markets, trains, church bells, rain on cobblestones. Could use the Zoom H1n recorder.

fields.title: Learn to juggle three balls
fields.notes: Apparently takes about 20 minutes of practice per day for a week. Good desk break activity.

fields.title: Host a dinner party with a theme
fields.notes: Maybe a 1920s theme, or a cuisine from a country I've never cooked. Dress code optional.

fields.title: Write a letter to my future self
fields.notes: Seal it and open in one year. FutureMe.org does digital versions, but handwritten feels better.

fields.title: Try a parkrun on Saturday morning
fields.notes: Free 5K timed run in the park. Every Saturday at 9 AM. Just need to register online.

fields.title: Build a terrarium in a glass jar
fields.notes: Closed ecosystem with moss, small ferns, and pebbles. Barely needs any maintenance.

fields.title: Explore the local second-hand bookshops properly
fields.notes: There are at least three I always walk past. Set aside an afternoon to browse each one.

fields.title: Learn to make proper espresso at home
fields.notes: Moka pot is fine, but maybe invest in a manual lever machine. Fresh beans from the local roaster.
